metadata.name: FACT_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This fact-based table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`). The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. The values in this table are not decimal adjusted, instead please use `core.dim_contracts` or `core.ez_token_transfers` to reference decimals or decimal adjusted values. This table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This will not be unique in this table as a transaction could include multiple transfer events.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: Contract address of the token being transferred.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

RAW_AMOUNT.name: RAW_AMOUNT
RAW_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of tokens transferred. This value is not decimal adjusted.

RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE
RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The amount of tokens transferred returned as a string to preserve precision. This value is not decimal adjusted.

_LOG_ID.name: _LOG_ID
_LOG_ID.comment: Deprecated. This column is no longer used. Please remove from your query by Jan. 31 2024.

FACT_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.name: FACT_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID
FACT_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_ASSET_METADATA
metadata.comment: A convenience table holding prioritized asset metadata and other relevant details pertaining to each token_address and native asset. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one unique asset per blockchain.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset. The case (upper / lower) may or may not be specified within the `dim_asset_metadata` table, as this column is raw and not transformed, coming directly from the provider APIs. However, in the `ez_` views, it will be lowercase by default for all EVMs.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the asset. May be NULL.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to the respective blockchain.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

EZ_ASSET_METADATA_ID.name: EZ_ASSET_METADATA_ID
EZ_ASSET_METADATA_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment: This table contains transaction level data for the Gnosis Blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash, along with transactions fees and an xDAI value transferred when applicable. Transactions may be native xDAI transfers or interactions with contract addresses. For more information, please see [The Ethereum Organization - Transactions](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/transactions/)

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

BLOCK_HASH.name: BLOCK_HASH
BLOCK_HASH.comment: Block hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generate when a block is produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: The number of transactions sent from a given address.

POSITION.name: POSITION
POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_FEE.name: TX_FEE
TX_FEE.comment: Amount paid to validate the transaction in xDAI.

TX_FEE_PRECISE.name: TX_FEE_PRECISE
TX_FEE_PRECISE.comment: The precise amount of the transaction fee. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

GAS_PRICE.name: GAS_PRICE
GAS_PRICE.comment: Cost per unit of gas in Gwei.

EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.name: EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE
EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.comment: The total base charge plus tip paid for each unit of gas, in Gwei.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Maximum amount of gas allocated for the transaction.

CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.name: CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED
CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.comment: The total amount of gas used when this transaction was executed in the block.

MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum priority fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

INPUT_DATA.name: INPUT_DATA
INPUT_DATA.comment: This column contains additional data for this transaction, and is commonly used as part of a contract interaction or as a message to the recipient.

R.name: R
R.comment: The r value of the transaction signature.

S.name: S
S.comment: The s value of the transaction signature.

V.name: V
V.comment: The v value of the transaction signature.

TX_TYPE.name: TX_TYPE
TX_TYPE.comment: The type of the transaction, 2 for EIP-1559 transactions and 0 for legacy transactions.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_LIQUIDATIONS
metadata.comment: This table contains transactions in which a borrower's collateral asset is liquidated across AAVE, REALT, SPARK, and AGAVE protocols. Liquidations occur when a borrower's health factor goes below 1 due to their collateral value not properly covering their loan/debt value. This might happen when the collateral decreases in value or the borrowed debt increases in value against each other. This collateral vs loan value ratio is shown in the health factor. In a liquidation, up to 50% of a borrower's debt is repaid and that value + liquidation fee is taken from the collateral available, so after a liquidation the amount liquidated from one's debt is repaid.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The decoded event name for a given event.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The specific protocol where lending event occurred.

LIQUIDATOR.name: LIQUIDATOR
LIQUIDATOR.comment: The address that initiated the liquidation call.

BORROWER.name: BORROWER
BORROWER.comment: Address that initiated the borrow event.

PROTOCOL_MARKET.name: PROTOCOL_MARKET
PROTOCOL_MARKET.comment: The protocol's specific lending asset token, ie cWBTC or aETHUni.

COLLATERAL_TOKEN.name: COLLATERAL_TOKEN
COLLATERAL_TOKEN.comment: The asset provided as collateral, which can be liquidated.

COLLATERAL_TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: COLLATERAL_TOKEN_SYMBOL
COLLATERAL_TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol belonging to the address of the token

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD

metadata.name: FACT_BLOCKS
metadata.comment: This table contains block level data for the Gnosis Blockchain. This table can be used to analyze trends at a block level, for example gas fees vs. total transactions over time. For more information on EVM transactions, please see [Etherscan Resources](https://etherscan.io/directory/Learning_Resources/Ethereum) or [The Ethereum Organization](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/blocks/)

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

NETWORK.name: NETWORK
NETWORK.comment: The network on the blockchain used by a transaction.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The blockchain on which transactions are being confirmed.

TX_COUNT.name: TX_COUNT
TX_COUNT.comment: Total number of transactions within a block.

DIFFICULTY.name: DIFFICULTY
DIFFICULTY.comment: The effort required to mine the block.

TOTAL_DIFFICULTY.name: TOTAL_DIFFICULTY
TOTAL_DIFFICULTY.comment: Total difficulty of the chain at a given block.

MINER.name: MINER
MINER.comment: Miner who successfully added a given block to the blockchain.

EXTRA_DATA.name: EXTRA_DATA
EXTRA_DATA.comment: Any data included by the validator for a given block.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Total gas limit provided by all transactions in the block.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: Total gas used in the block.

HASH.name: HASH
HASH.comment: The hash of the block header for a given block.

PARENT_HASH.name: PARENT_HASH
PARENT_HASH.comment: The hash of the block from which a given block is generated. Also known as the parent block.

RECEIPTS_ROOT.name: RECEIPTS_ROOT
RECEIPTS_ROOT.comment: The root of the state trie.

SHA3_UNCLES.name: SHA3_UNCLES
SHA3_UNCLES.comment: The mechanism which Ethereum Javascript RLP encodes an empty string.

SIZE.name: SIZE
SIZE.comment: Block size, which is determined by a given block's gas limit.

UNCLE_BLOCKS.name: UNCLE_BLOCKS
UNCLE_BLOCKS.comment: Uncle blocks occur when two blocks are mined and broadcasted at the same time, with the same block number. The block validated across the most nodes will be added to the primary chain, and the other one becomes an uncle block. Miners do receive rewards for uncle blocks.

BLOCK_HEADER_JSON.name: BLOCK_HEADER_JSON
BLOCK_HEADER_JSON.comment: This JSON column contains the block header details.

FACT_BLOCKS_ID.name: FACT_BLOCKS_ID
FACT_BLOCKS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_FLASHLOANS
metadata.comment: This table contains flash loan transactions across AAVE, SPARK, and AGAVE protocols. Flash loans are a feature designed for developers, due to the technical knowledge required to execute one. Flash Loans allow you to borrow any available amount of assets without providing any collateral, as long as the liquidity is returned to the protocol within one block transaction.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The decoded event name for a given event.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The specific protocol where lending event occurred.

INITIATOR.name: INITIATOR
INITIATOR.comment: The address that initiated the flash loan.

TARGET.name: TARGET
TARGET.comment: The address receiving the flash loan.

PROTOCOL_MARKET.name: PROTOCOL_MARKET
PROTOCOL_MARKET.comment: The protocol's specific lending asset token, ie cWBTC or aETHUni.

FLASHLOAN_TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: FLASHLOAN_TOKEN_SYMBOL
FLASHLOAN_TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol belonging to the address of the token

FLASHLOAN_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: FLASHLOAN_AMOUNT_UNADJ
FLASHLOAN_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The amount of assets flash loaned, non-decimal adjusted.

FLASHLOAN_AMOUNT.name: FLASHLOAN_AMOUNT
FLASHLOAN_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of assets flash loaned.

FLASHLOAN_AMOUNT_USD.name: FLASHLOAN_AMOUNT_USD
FLASHLOAN_AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the flash loan amount, in USD.

PREMIUM_AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: PREMIUM_AMOUNT_UNADJ
PREMIUM_AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The flash loan fee, changeable via the normal governance process, non-decimal adjusted.

PREMIUM_AMOUNT.name: PREMIUM_AMOUNT
PREMIUM_AMOUNT.comment: The flash loan fee, changeable via the normal governance process, decimal adjusted.

PREMIUM_AMOUNT_USD.name: PREMIUM_AMOUNT_USD
PREMIUM_AMOUNT_USD.comment: The flash loan fee, valued in USD.

EZ_LENDING_FLASHLOANS_ID.name: EZ_LENDING_FLASHLOANS_ID
EZ_LENDING_FLASHLOANS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment:
This table currently contains swap events from the ```fact_event_logs``` table for CURVE, BALANCER, SUSHI, HONEYSWAP, and SWAPR along with other helpful columns including an amount USD where possible. Other dexes coming soon! 
Note: A rule has been put in place to null out the amount_USD if that number is too divergent between amount_in_usd and amount_out_usd. This can happen for swaps of less liquid tokens during very high fluctuation of price.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The decoded event name for a given event.

AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ
AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN.name: AMOUNT_IN
AMOUNT_IN.comment: The amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN_USD.name: AMOUNT_IN_USD
AMOUNT_IN_USD.comment: The value of the swapped tokens in USD at the time of the transaction, where available. If there is a variance of 75% or greater in USD swap values or one side of the swap's USD value is NULL, then both amount_in_usd and amount_out_usd will be set to NULL. This avoids falsely overstating USD swap volumes for low-liquidity token pairings or incorrectly reported prices. To get USD values in these scenarios anyway, please re-join the `price.ez_prices_hourly` table. Note, this logic does not apply for the chain's Native asset.

AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT.name: AMOUNT_OUT
AMOUNT_OUT.comment: The amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT_USD.name: AMOUNT_OUT_USD
AMOUNT_OUT_USD.comment: The value of the swapped tokens in USD at the time of the transaction, where available. If there is a variance of 75% or greater in USD swap values or one side of the swap's USD value is NULL, then both amount_in_usd and amount_out_usd will be set to NULL. This avoids falsely overstating USD swap volumes for low-liquidity token pairings or incorrectly reported prices. To get USD values in these scenarios anyway, please re-join the `price.ez_prices_hourly` table. Note, this logic does not apply for the chain's Native asset.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The router is the sender in the swap function.

TX_TO.name: TX_TO
TX_TO.comment: The tx_to is the address who receives the swapped token. This corresponds to the "to" field in the swap function.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol or platform that the liquidity pool belongs to or swap occurred on.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment: The address of the token sent for swap.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment: The address of the token being swapped to.

SYMBOL_IN.name: SYMBOL_IN
SYMBOL_IN.comment: The symbol of the token sent for swap.

SYMBOL_OUT.name: SYMBOL_OUT
SYMBOL_OUT.comment: The symbol of the token being swapped to.

_LOG_ID.name: _LOG_ID
_LOG_ID.comment: Deprecated. This column is no longer used. Please remove from your query by Jan. 31 2024.

EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID
EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
'For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for. This table will perform better than the `core__ez_decoded_event_logs` table, but does not include as many columns.  The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters. You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query. The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
Sample query for USDC Transfer events: ```sql select  tx_hash, block_number, contract_address, decoded_log:from::string as from_address, decoded_log:to::string as to_address, decoded_log:value::integer as value from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48') and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000 and event_name = 'Transfer' limit 50```'

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

DECODED_LOG.name: DECODED_LOG
DECODED_LOG.comment: The flattened decoded log, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.name: FULL_DECODED_LOG
FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

FACT_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: FACT_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID
FACT_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_REPAYMENTS
metadata.comment: This table contains transactions in which a borrower repays their loan (debt) across the AAVE, REALT, SPARK, and AGAVE protocols.  Loans are repaid in the same asset borrowed, plus accrued interest. Borrowers can pay back their loan based on the USD price as they can borrow any of the available stable coins (USDC, DAI, USDT, etc.).

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The decoded event name for a given event.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The specific protocol where lending event occurred.

PAYER.name: PAYER
PAYER.comment: The address that initiated the repayment.

BORROWER.name: BORROWER
BORROWER.comment: Address that initiated the borrow event.

PROTOCOL_MARKET.name: PROTOCOL_MARKET
PROTOCOL_MARKET.comment: The protocol's specific lending asset token, ie cWBTC or aETHUni.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the token associated with the lending action.

TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol of the token associated with the lending action.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction, where available.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the tokens in USD at the time of the lending transaction, where available.

EZ_LENDING_REPAYMENTS_ID.name: EZ_LENDING_REPAYMENTS_ID
EZ_LENDING_REPAYMENTS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_WITHDRAWS
metadata.comment: This table contains transactions in which a user withdraws liquidity across the AAVE, REALT, SPARK, and AGAVE protocols. Users need to make sure there is enough liquidity (not borrowed) in order to withdraw, if this is not the case, users need to wait for more liquidity from depositors or borrowers repaying.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The decoded event name for a given event.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The specific protocol where lending event occurred.

DEPOSITOR.name: DEPOSITOR
DEPOSITOR.comment: Its the address of the user who is Borrowing or repaying the loan, depending on the action.

PROTOCOL_MARKET.name: PROTOCOL_MARKET
PROTOCOL_MARKET.comment: The protocol's specific lending asset token, ie cWBTC or aETHUni.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the token associated with the lending action.

TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol of the token associated with the lending action.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction, where available.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the tokens in USD at the time of the lending transaction, where available.

EZ_LENDING_WITHDRAWS_ID.name: EZ_LENDING_WITHDRAWS_ID
EZ_LENDING_WITHDRAWS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_LABELS
metadata.comment: The labels table is a store of one-to-one address identifiers, or an address name. Labels are broken out into a "type" (such as cex, dex, dapp, games, etc.) and a "subtype" (ex: contract_deployer, hot_wallet, token_contract, etc.) in order to help classify each address name into similar groups. Our labels are sourced from many different places, but can primarily be grouped into two categories: automatic and manual. Automatic labels are continuously labeled based on certain criteria, such as a known contract deploying another contract, behavior based algorithms for finding deposit wallets, and consistent data pulls of custom protocol APIs. Manual labels are done periodically to find addresses that cannot be found programmatically such as finding new protocol addresses, centralized exchange hot wallets, or trending addresses. Labels can also be added by our community by using our add-a-label tool (https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/add-a-label/) or on-chain with near (https://near.social/lord1.near/widget/Form) and are reviewed by our labels team. A label can be removed by our labels team if it is found to be incorrect or no longer relevant; this generally will only happen for mislabeled deposit wallets.

CREATOR.name: CREATOR
CREATOR.comment: The name of the creator of the label.

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: Address that the label is for. This is the field that should be used to join other tables with labels.

ADDRESS_NAME.name: ADDRESS_NAME
ADDRESS_NAME.comment: This table contains labels for addresses on the Gnosis Blockchain.

LABEL_TYPE.name: LABEL_TYPE
LABEL_TYPE.comment: A high-level category describing the addresses main function or ownership.

LABEL_SUBTYPE.name: LABEL_SUBTYPE
LABEL_SUBTYPE.comment: A sub-category nested within label type providing further detail.

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment: The name of the project for this address.

DIM_LABELS_ID.name: DIM_LABELS_ID
DIM_LABELS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_CONTRACT_ABIS
metadata.comment: 'This table contains the contract ABIs that we have sourced from Gnosisscan, the community, or bytecode matched. This table is the source of ABIs used in the `core__ez_decoded_event_logs` and `core__fact_decoded_event_logs` tables. We first try to source ABIs from Gnosisscan. If we cannot find an ABI on Gnosisscan, we will rely on user submissions. To add a contract to this table, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). If we are unable to locate an ABI for a contract from Gnosisscan or the community, we will try to find an ABI to use by matching the contract bytecode to a known contract bytecode we do have an ABI for.'

ABI.name: ABI
ABI.comment: The JSON ABI for the contract.

ABI_SOURCE.name: ABI_SOURCE
ABI_SOURCE.comment: The source of the ABI. This can be `Gnosisscan`, `user_submitted`, or `bytecode_matched`.

BYTECODE.name: BYTECODE
BYTECODE.comment: The deployed bytecode of the contract.

metadata.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains all transfers for the chain's native asset, sourced from internal traces (`core.fact_traces`), and includes decimal adjusted and usd values where available. The origin addresses correspond to the to and from addresses in the `core.fact_transactions` table. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This will not be unique in this table as a transaction could include multiple transfer events.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

TRACE_INDEX.name: TRACE_INDEX
TRACE_INDEX.comment: The index of the trace within the transaction.

IDENTIFIER.name: IDENTIFIER
IDENTIFIER.comment: This field represents the position and type of the trace within the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

AMOUNT_PRECISE_RAW.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE_RAW
AMOUNT_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID
EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

metadata.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates bridge activity from event_logs, traces and transfers, including bridge deposits and transfers sent from the following protocols: CELER, HOP, MESON along with other helpful columns, including an amount USD where available. Note, this table only includes records for the protocols listed above with live, onchain bridge activity and may not represent the complete bridging picture.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address where the transaction originated from. This may be an EOA or contract address, however in most cases this is the user that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract responsible for handling the bridge deposit or transfer. This contract mediates the transfer and ensures that assets are sent and received appropriately.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The decoded event name for a given event.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The platform or protocol from which the bridge transaction or event originates.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The address that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer. This address is the sender of the tokens/assets being bridged to the destination chain. This may be an EOA or contract address.

RECEIVER.name: RECEIVER
RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the deposit or transfer. This may be an EOA or contract address.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER
DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the bridged tokens on the target chain after the completion of the bridge transaction. For non-evm chains, the hex address is decoded/encoded to match the data format of the destination chain, where possible. This may be an EOA or contract address.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain network to which the assets are being bridged. It could be any EVM compatible chain or other blockchain networks that the bridging protocol supports.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID
DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.comment: The numeric identifier associated with the destination blockchain network. This is specific to the chain and helps in uniquely identifying it.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address associated with the token that is being bridged. It provides a unique identifier for the token within its origin blockchain.

TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol representing the token being bridged. This provides a shorthand representation of the token.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The raw, non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the bridge transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the bridge transaction, where available.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the bridged tokens in USD at the time of the bridge transaction, where available.

EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID
EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_ASSET_METADATA
metadata.comment: A comprehensive dimensional table holding asset metadata and other relevant details pertaining to each id, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this dimensional model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_asset_metadata.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset. The case (upper / lower) may or may not be specified within the `dim_asset_metadata` table, as this column is raw and not transformed, coming directly from the provider APIs. However, in the `ez_` views, it will be lowercase by default for all EVMs.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

BLOCKCHAIN_ID.name: BLOCKCHAIN_ID
BLOCKCHAIN_ID.comment: The unique identifier of the Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

PROVIDER.name: PROVIDER
PROVIDER.comment: The provider or source of the data.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

DIM_ASSET_METADATA_ID.name: DIM_ASSET_METADATA_ID
DIM_ASSET_METADATA_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: DIM_CONTRACTS
metadata.comment: This table contains all the contracts that are deployed on the Gnosis blockchain along with their on-chain metadata.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals used to adjust amount for this contract.

CREATED_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: CREATED_BLOCK_NUMBER
CREATED_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number when the contract was created

CREATED_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: CREATED_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
CREATED_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp when the contract was created

CREATED_TX_HASH.name: CREATED_TX_HASH
CREATED_TX_HASH.comment: The transaction hash when the contract was created

CREATOR_ADDRESS.name: CREATOR_ADDRESS
CREATOR_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the creator of the contract

DIM_CONTRACTS_ID.name: DIM_CONTRACTS_ID
DIM_CONTRACTS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP

metadata.name: DIM_DEX_LIQUIDITY_POOLS
metadata.comment: This table contains details on decentralized exchange (DEX) liquidity pools (LP) on the Gnosis blockchain, including the tokens, symbols and decimals within each pool alongside the following protocols: CURVE, BALANCER, SUSHI, HONEYSWAP, and SWAPR.

CREATION_BLOCK.name: CREATION_BLOCK
CREATION_BLOCK.comment: The block number of when this pool was created.

CREATION_TIME.name: CREATION_TIME
CREATION_TIME.comment: The block timestamp of when this pool was created.

CREATION_TX.name: CREATION_TX
CREATION_TX.comment: The transaction where this contract was created.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol or platform that the liquidity pool belongs to or swap occurred on.

FACTORY_ADDRESS.name: FACTORY_ADDRESS
FACTORY_ADDRESS.comment: The address that created or deployed this pool, where available.

POOL_ADDRESS.name: POOL_ADDRESS
POOL_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address for the liquidity pool.

POOL_NAME.name: POOL_NAME
POOL_NAME.comment: The name of the liquidity pool, where available. In some cases, the pool name is a concatenation of symbols or token addresses.

TOKENS.name: TOKENS
TOKENS.comment:
The address for the token included in the liquidity pool, as a JSON object. 

Query example to access the key:value pairing within the object:
SELECT
    DISTINCT pool_address AS unique_pools,
    tokens :token0 :: STRING AS token0,
    symbols: token0 :: STRING AS token0_symbol,
    decimals: token0 :: STRING AS token0_decimal
FROM gnosis.defi.dim_dex_liquidity_pools
WHERE token0 = '0x6a023ccd1ff6f2045c3309768ead9e68f978f6e1'
;

SYMBOLS.name: SYMBOLS
SYMBOLS.comment:
The symbol for the token included in the liquidity pool, as a JSON object, where available. 

Query example to access the key:value pairing within the object:
SELECT
    DISTINCT pool_address AS unique_pools,
    tokens :token0 :: STRING AS token0,
    symbols: token0 :: STRING AS token0_symbol,
    decimals: token0 :: STRING AS token0_decimal
FROM gnosis.defi.dim_dex_liquidity_pools
WHERE token0_symbol = 'WETH'
;

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment:
The # of decimals for the token included in the liquidity pool, as a JSON object, where available. 

Query example to access the key:value pairing within the object:
SELECT
    DISTINCT pool_address AS unique_pools,
    tokens :token0 :: STRING AS token0,
    symbols: token0 :: STRING AS token0_symbol,
    decimals: token0 :: STRING AS token0_decimal
FROM gnosis.defi.dim_dex_liquidity_pools
WHERE token0_decimal = 6
;

DIM_DEX_LIQUIDITY_POOLS_ID.name: DIM_DEX_LIQUIDITY_POOLS_ID
DIM_DEX_LIQUIDITY_POOLS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_DEPOSITS
metadata.comment: This table contains deposit transactions across AAVE, REALT, SPARK, and AGAVE protocols. A user deposits their preferred asset and amount. After depositing, users earn passive income based on the market borrowing demand. Additionally, depositing allows users to borrow by using their deposited assets as a collateral. Any interest earned by depositing funds helps offset the interest rate accumulated by borrowing.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The decoded event name for a given event.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The specific protocol where lending event occurred.

PROTOCOL_MARKET.name: PROTOCOL_MARKET
PROTOCOL_MARKET.comment: The protocol's specific lending asset token, ie cWBTC or aETHUni.

DEPOSITOR.name: DEPOSITOR
DEPOSITOR.comment: Address that initiated a deposit event.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the token associated with the lending action.

TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol belonging to the address of the token

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction, where available.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the tokens in USD at the time of the lending transaction, where available.

EZ_LENDING_DEPOSITS_ID.name: EZ_LENDING_DEPOSITS_ID
EZ_LENDING_DEPOSITS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_NFT_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This table contains NFT transfer events on the Gnosis blockchain.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp at which the NFT event occurred.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number at which the NFT event occurred.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: The transaction hash for the NFT event. This is not necessarily unique in this table as a transaction may contain multiple NFT events.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The event number within a transaction

INTRA_EVENT_INDEX.name: INTRA_EVENT_INDEX
INTRA_EVENT_INDEX.comment: The order of events within a single event index. This is primarily used for ERC1155 NFT batch transfer events.

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The type of NFT event in this transaction, either `sale`,  `bid_won` or `redeem`.

NFT_ADDRESS.name: NFT_ADDRESS
NFT_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address of the NFT.

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment: The name of the NFT project. This field, along with metadata, will be filled in over time.

NFT_FROM_ADDRESS.name: NFT_FROM_ADDRESS
NFT_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of the NFT in the transaction.

NFT_TO_ADDRESS.name: NFT_TO_ADDRESS
NFT_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of the NFT in the transaction.

TOKENID.name: TOKENID
TOKENID.comment: The token ID for this NFT contract.

ERC1155_VALUE.name: ERC1155_VALUE
ERC1155_VALUE.comment: If the NFT is an ERC-1155 contract, this field may be one or greater, representing the number of tokens. If it is not an ERC-1155 token, this value will be null.

EZ_NFT_TRANSFERS_ID.name: EZ_NFT_TRANSFERS_ID
EZ_NFT_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
'For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for. The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters. You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query. The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
      
Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
```sql select  tx_hash, block_number, contract_address, decoded_log:from::string as from_address, decoded_log:to::string as to_address, decoded_log:value::integer as value from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48') and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000 and event_name = 'Transfer' limit 50```'

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The name of the contract, if the contract has a name() function.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

DECODED_LOG.name: DECODED_LOG
DECODED_LOG.comment: The flattened decoded log, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.name: FULL_DECODED_LOG
FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID
EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

row_count.label: Row Count
row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

bytes.label: Approximate Size
bytes.description: Approximate size of the table as reported by Snowflake

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: FACT_TRACES
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened trace data for internal contract calls on the Gnosis Blockchain. Hex encoded fields can be decoded to integers by using `utils.udf_hex_to_int()`.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: The transaction hash for the trace. Please note, this is not necessarily unique in this table as transactions frequently have multiple traces.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number of this transaction.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp of this transaction.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this trace. This is not necessarily the from address of the transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this trace. This is not necessarily the to address of the transaction.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The amount of xDAI transferred in this trace.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

GAS.name: GAS
GAS.comment: The gas supplied for this trace.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: The gas used for this trace.

INPUT.name: INPUT
INPUT.comment: The input data for this trace.

OUTPUT.name: OUTPUT
OUTPUT.comment: The output data for this trace.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The type of internal transaction. Common trace types are `CALL`, `DELEGATECALL`, and `STATICCALL`.

IDENTIFIER.name: IDENTIFIER
IDENTIFIER.comment: This field represents the position and type of the trace within the transaction.

DATA.name: DATA
DATA.comment: The raw JSON data for this trace.

SUB_TRACES.name: SUB_TRACES
SUB_TRACES.comment: The amount of nested sub traces for this trace.

TRACE_STATUS.name: TRACE_STATUS
TRACE_STATUS.comment: The status of the trace, either `SUCCESS` or `FAIL`

ERROR_REASON.name: ERROR_REASON
ERROR_REASON.comment: The reason for the trace failure, if any.

TRACE_INDEX.name: TRACE_INDEX
TRACE_INDEX.comment: The index of the trace within the transaction.

FACT_TRACES_ID.name: FACT_TRACES_ID
FACT_TRACES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table for determining token prices by address and blockchain, and native asset prices by symbol and blockchain. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one price per hour per unique asset and blockchain.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset. The case (upper / lower) may or may not be specified within the `dim_asset_metadata` table, as this column is raw and not transformed, coming directly from the provider APIs. However, in the `ez_` views, it will be lowercase by default for all EVMs.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the asset. May be NULL.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to the respective blockchain.

IS_IMPUTED.name: IS_IMPUTED
IS_IMPUTED.comment: A flag indicating if the price was imputed, or derived, from the last arriving record. This is generally used for tokens with low-liquidity or inconsistent reporting.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID
EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates block and transaction related metrics using various aggregate functions such as SUM, COUNT, MIN and MAX from the fact_transactions table, on an hourly basis.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.comment: The hour of the timestamp of the block.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN
BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.comment: The minimum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX
BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.comment: The maximum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_COUNT.name: BLOCK_COUNT
BLOCK_COUNT.comment: The number of blocks in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS
TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.comment: The number of successful transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED
TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.comment: The number of failed transactions in the hour.

UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT
UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.comment: The number of unique origin from addresses in the hour.

UNIQUE_TO_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_TO_COUNT
UNIQUE_TO_COUNT.comment: The number of unique origin to addresses in the hour.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in USD, rounded to 2 decimal places.

EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID
EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A comprehensive fact table holding id and provider specific open, high, low, close hourly prices, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this fact based model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_prices_hourly.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

OPEN.name: OPEN
OPEN.comment: Opening price of the recorded hour in USD.

HIGH.name: HIGH
HIGH.comment: Highest price of the recorded hour in USD

LOW.name: LOW
LOW.comment: Lowest price of the recorded hour in USD

CLOSE.name: CLOSE
CLOSE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID.name: FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID
FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on the Gnosis Blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

_LOG_ID.name: _LOG_ID
_LOG_ID.comment: Deprecated. This column is no longer used. Please remove from your query by Jan. 31 2024.

FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID
FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, usd values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This will not be unique in this table as a transaction could include multiple transfer events.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: Contract address of the token being transferred.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE
RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The amount of tokens transferred returned as a string to preserve precision. This value is not decimal adjusted.

RAW_AMOUNT.name: RAW_AMOUNT
RAW_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of tokens transferred. This value is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token returned as a string to preserve precision. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The amount in US dollars for this transfer at the time of the transfer. Tokens without a decimal adjustment or price will be nulled out here.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimal places this contract needs adjusted where token values exist. For example, use the decimal field to correctly transform raw amounts in ```fact_token_transfers```.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The symbol belonging to the address of the token

TOKEN_PRICE.name: TOKEN_PRICE
TOKEN_PRICE.comment: The price, if available, for this token at the transfer time.

HAS_DECIMAL.name: HAS_DECIMAL
HAS_DECIMAL.comment: Whether or not our contracts model contains the necessary decimal adjustment for this token.

HAS_PRICE.name: HAS_PRICE
HAS_PRICE.comment: Whether or not our prices model contains this hourly token price.

_LOG_ID.name: _LOG_ID
_LOG_ID.comment: Deprecated. This column is no longer used. Please remove from your query by Jan. 31 2024.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Deprecated. This column is no longer used. Please remove from your query by Jan. 31 2024.

EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID
EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_BORROWS
metadata.comment: This table contains transactions where users borrowed assets across AAVE, REALT, SPARK, and AGAVE protocols. In order to borrow assets, a user must first deposit their preferred asset and amount as collateral.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The decoded event name for a given event.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The specific protocol where lending event occurred.

PROTOCOL_MARKET.name: PROTOCOL_MARKET
PROTOCOL_MARKET.comment: The protocol's specific lending asset token, ie cWBTC or aETHUni.

BORROWER.name: BORROWER
BORROWER.comment: Address that initiated the borrow event.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the token associated with the lending action.

TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol belonging to the address of the token

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction, where available.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the tokens in USD at the time of the lending transaction, where available.

EZ_LENDING_BORROWS_ID.name: EZ_LENDING_BORROWS_ID
EZ_LENDING_BORROWS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS
metadata.comment: This table contains the individual event abis for each all contracts that we have ABIs for. Proxies are rolled up to their implementation.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The blockchain that the event abi is for.

PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS
PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event abi is for.

SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME.name: SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME
SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event along with its parameters.

EVENT_SIGNATURE.name: EVENT_SIGNATURE
EVENT_SIGNATURE.comment: The keccak256 signature of the simple event name. This can be used to filter for events in the logs table on topics[0].

START_BLOCK.name: START_BLOCK
START_BLOCK.comment: The block number of the creation of the contract containing the event. This could be from a proxy or an implementation.

END_BLOCK.name: END_BLOCK
END_BLOCK.comment: The block number before a similar event was created in the contract. This could be from a proxy or an implementation.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS_ID.name: DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS_ID
DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?

metadata.name: DIM_LABELS
metadata.comment: The labels table is a store of one-to-one address identifiers, or an address name. Labels are broken out into a "type" (such as cex, dex, dapp, games, etc.) and a "subtype" (ex: contract_deployer, hot_wallet, token_contract, etc.) in order to help classify each address name into similar groups. Our labels are sourced from many different places, but can primarily be grouped into two categories: automatic and manual. Automatic labels are continuously labeled based on certain criteria, such as a known contract deploying another contract, behavior based algorithms for finding deposit wallets, and consistent data pulls of custom protocol APIs. Manual labels are done periodically to find addresses that cannot be found programatically such as finding new protocol addresses, centralized exchange hot wallets, or trending addresses. Labels can also be added by our community by using our add-a-label tool (https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/add-a-label/) or on-chain with near (https://near.social/lord1.near/widget/Form) and are reviewed by our labels team. A label can be removed by our labels team if it is found to be incorrect or no longer relevant; this generally will only happen for mislabeled deposit wallets.

INSERT_DATE.name: INSERT_DATE
INSERT_DATE.comment: The date the label was inserted in the table

ADDRESS.comment: Address that the label is for. This is the field that should be used to join other tables with labels.

CREATOR.name: CREATOR
CREATOR.comment: The name of the creator of the label

LABEL_TYPE.comment: A high-level category describing the addresses main function or ownership

LABEL_SUBTYPE.name: LABEL_SUBTYPE
LABEL_SUBTYPE.comment: A sub-category nested within label type providing further detail

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME

DIM_LABELS_ID.name: DIM_LABELS_ID
DIM_LABELS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.
